title: Sprite Conformance And Variation Pipeline
summary: Build one repeatable sprite pipeline that can reach high measured reference conformance, then branch into production-safe Aurora and future-game visual themes without losing the era, motion, and gameplay readability characteristics of the source.

principles.label: Separate target conformance from shipped production art
principles.meaning: The ingestion system should learn and score against target sprites, poses, motion, and gameplay meaning. Production builds can ship original Aurora-style variations that preserve measured silhouette, role readability, timing, and era characteristics.

principles.label: Strict conformance mode is a development/lab lane
principles.meaning: Developer settings should eventually let us run a conforming reference-experience lane for measurement, comparison, and review. That lane is not the default public creative skin unless rights and release policy allow it.

principles.label: Sprite identity is temporal, not static
principles.meaning: A high-quality arcade sprite includes static pose, flap cadence, damage pulse, dive and rotation poses, capture/rescue transitions, projectile vocabulary, explosion language, scale, palette, and formation context.

principles.label: Platinum supports the pipeline, games own their assets
principles.meaning: Platinum should provide renderer modes, developer toggles, artifact wiring, replay capture, and harness APIs. Aurora and each future game should own its target mappings, conforming lane, and creative theme variants.

lanes.label: Reference Conformance Lane
lanes.purpose: Show and measure the closest legally appropriate Galaga-like game experience so the team can assess what conformance would look like before creative variation.
lanes.rendererExpectation: Fixed low-resolution gameplay buffer, integer or controlled scaling, no smoothing, target-crop sprite atlases or target-derived pixel-row manifests, authored pose frames instead of arbitrary canvas rotation.

lanes.label: Aurora Production Theme Lane
lanes.purpose: Ship original Aurora art that feels era-faithful and arcade-readable while avoiding a direct copy of target art.
lanes.rendererExpectation: Uses the same sprite-role/pose manifest shape as the conformance lane, but with Aurora-owned pixel art, palettes, effects, and themed variations.
metrics: player comprehension during hits, captures, rescues, and challenge stages

lanes.label: Future Game Ingestion Lane
lanes.purpose: Use the same source-to-target-to-variation loop for Galaxy Guardians and later games so new games begin with measured artifacts, not hand-waved design memory.
lanes.rendererExpectation: Game-owned target packs and themed packs share the same manifest schema, crop review, scorer, and developer toggle vocabulary.

pipelineSteps.label: Source Manifest
pipelineSteps.goal: Register lawful target sources and classify them as scoring targets, human-review context, secondary validation, or planning-only references.
pipelineSteps.currentStatus: Galaga alien close-ups are ingested and classified; general sprite sheet is the strongest current static target candidate; a segmented alien motion/pulse video is now registered as a target-validation and temporal-reference source.
pipelineSteps.nextAction: Keep source authority explicit when new sprite sheets, gameplay clips, posters, or manual screenshots enter the project.

pipelineSteps.label: Crop-Box Manifest
pipelineSteps.goal: Define bounding boxes for player, bee, butterfly, boss, captured fighter, projectiles, tractor beam, explosions, challenge aliens, and each important pose frame.
pipelineSteps.currentStatus: First-pass region manifest exists at reference-artifacts/ingestion/galaga-alien-visual-reference/crop-box-manifest-0.1.json, generated review previews exist at reference-artifacts/analyses/galaga-alien-visual-crop-previews/latest.json, and per-role/per-pose target crops now exist at reference-artifacts/analyses/galaga-alien-target-crops/latest.json. Human review found polluted crop evidence for the original boss/bee/butterfly target rows, so the library now separates trusted cleaned motion-reference crops from provisional source-sheet cells and records that decision in reference-artifacts/analyses/galaga-target-evidence-audit/latest.json.
pipelineSteps.nextAction: Regenerate runtime-vs-target scoring against the trusted boss, bee, and butterfly crops, then promote clean player-fighter and temporal pulse-pair targets before making additional runtime sprite changes.

pipelineSteps.label: Pose And Motion Targets
pipelineSteps.goal: Group static crops into temporal identities: flap cycles, dive turns, boss damage, capture/rescue, dual fighter, projectiles, and explosions.
pipelineSteps.currentStatus: Static model and runtime crop scoring exist, and a 33-crop/7-role promoted target library now supplies multi-pose static targets. Primary Boss/Bee/Butterfly/Fighter crops are trusted cleaned motion-reference targets; remaining source-sheet cells are provisional until recropped or replaced by temporal windows. Seed runtime windows now cover all four planned motion axes: flap phase pairs, full flap cadence, dive/rotation poses, capture/carry/dual transitions, and first-pass impact/explosion event crops. A segmented alien motion/pulse video now provides cleaner human-readable pulse/cadence evidence for Boss, Butterfly, Bee, specialty aliens, Fighter, and Tractor Beam. Temporal target rows now exist at reference-artifacts/analyses/galaga-alien-temporal-targets/latest.json, with Boss stronger than Bee/Butterfly because the latter still depend on provisional flap cells.
pipelineSteps.nextAction: Replace provisional Bee and Butterfly flap cells with clean frame-timed target windows, then score Aurora runtime cadence against those target sequences.

pipelineSteps.label: Runtime Renderer Path
pipelineSteps.goal: Render sprites through a fixed-buffer, smoothing-free, artifact-driven path that supports target mode and creative theme variants.
pipelineSteps.currentStatus: Current canvas path is DPR-aware and smoothing-free, but sprites are still compact hand-authored patterns and responsive scaling complicates pixel scoring.
pipelineSteps.nextAction: Prototype a sprite atlas/pixel-row renderer behind a development flag before changing normal gameplay.

pipelineSteps.label: Scoring And Gates
pipelineSteps.goal: Score static crops, active motion, stage context, player meaning, and shipped-theme originality separately.
pipelineSteps.currentStatus: Runtime static sprite score is 5.75/10 against inferred models while the stricter runtime-vs-promoted-target-crop comparator now reads 4.33/10 after replacing polluted Boss/Bee/Butterfly targets with trusted motion-reference crops. That score decrease is useful: it removes false confidence and exposes the direct visible geometry gap. Impact/explosion feedback has a first-pass static crop comparator at 5.86/10 after adding a target-derived pixel explosion layer. Active sprite motion has seed windows but is not yet target-frame temporal scoring.
pipelineSteps.nextAction: Move direct target sprite score toward 5.5+ before the next beta claim, keep challenge-stage set-piece conformance above 5.0 before claiming a beta conformance lift, and add confidence/resolution plus temporal target scoring.

pipelineSteps.label: Variation Generation
pipelineSteps.goal: Use artists, scripts, model-assisted generation, or tools such as agent-sprite-forge to create era-faithful original variants after the target lane is understood.
pipelineSteps.currentStatus: Candidate tooling noted but not adopted; generated assets should be treated as candidates until reviewed, cropped, scored, and committed.
pipelineSteps.nextAction: Evaluate candidate generation tools against the same manifest and scoring shape, not as a replacement for source-grounded targets.

pipelineSteps.label: Developer Controls
pipelineSteps.goal: Expose conforming mode, Aurora theme mode, and future-game theme variants through developer settings for visual comparison and evidence capture.
pipelineSteps.currentStatus: Developer graphics/audio settings exist, but no formal sprite-conformance lane toggle exists yet.
pipelineSteps.nextAction: Add a future developer setting for Sprite Mode: Reference Conformance, Aurora Production, and Theme Variant once target packs exist.

successCriteria.label: Short Term
successCriteria.target: Crop-box manifest exists for the strongest Galaga sprite sheet, and the Application Guide shows target crops beside Aurora runtime crops.

successCriteria.label: Medium Term
successCriteria.target: Runtime sprite scoring compares multiple poses per role and moves from static-only 6.2/10 toward 7.5+/10 without overstating motion coverage.

successCriteria.label: Release Gate
successCriteria.target: Before the next beta conformance claim, strict direct target sprite conformance should be at least 5.5/10 and challenge-stage set-piece conformance should be at least 5.0/10. Before broad public promotion, production theme sprites are original, readable, era-faithful, and backed by conformance-lane evidence plus variation-distance notes.

successCriteria.label: Long Term
successCriteria.target: The same sprite ingestion, scoring, and variation pipeline can onboard a new Platinum game with minimal bespoke authoring.

artifacts.label: Runtime versus promoted Galaga target crops

nextBestStep: Promote a clean player-fighter target-crop, add temporal pulse-pair targets for Boss/Bee/Butterfly, then tune Aurora runtime-vs-target sprite geometry against the corrected target library before the next beta handoff.
